focus: Compare explicit slug maximum-length validity requirements against implementation truncation behavior

findingDetails.title: Truncation can leave a trailing separator
findingDetails.evidence: Spec requires no edge separators and truncation that keeps the slug valid; implementation strips edge hyphens before slicing and does not repair the sliced result.
findingDetails.recommendation: After truncation, remove edge separators again or truncate to the last safe alphanumeric boundary within maxLen.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Searched the fixture for slugify/maxLen/truncation handling; only src/slugify.js implements slugify and no post-slice repair or wrapper was present.

noveltyJustification: First iteration against an empty registry; one new P1 spec-code correctness mismatch contributes all current weighted finding signal.

traceabilityChecks.evidence: tasks.md:5-8 claims max-length enforcement complete but implementation evidence contradicts full compliance.

edgeCases: Fixture intentionally contains seeded imperfections; findings are for benchmark review only, not code modification.
resource-map.md absent; resource-map coverage not applicable.
maxIterations=1 leaves security, traceability, and maintainability mostly unreviewed.
ruledOut: Non-string input validation escalation: spec.md scopes expected inputs to short title strings.
P0 severity: deterministic spec mismatch without immediate security or destructive-data impact.
